title: Échange App
video_url: Highly specific search: 'Collaborative software design and peer review in decentralized systems'
sources: README.md: Community alignment and consensus-building objectives
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District F
database.sql: Memo archival and social role tracking

resume: The quest 'Échange App' (F1) represents the first collaborative feedback loop for the software architecture within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. Located at the intersection of District F (Logiciel) and the 'Échange' action (1), this quest is the essential stage of opening the floor for dialogue and peer review regarding the functional requirements established in F0. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), the mission to 'Save human communities and long-term memory' requires software that is not just technically sound but also battle-tested by the collective experience of its early users. This exchange serves as the formal mechanism for developers, moderators, and end-users to share insights on the app's usability, performance, and sovereign features. Technically, every contribution to this software dialogue is archived in the local PostgreSQL database within the 'memos' table, indexed under Category F and Question 1. This ensures that the history of the community’s technical evolution is preserved as sovereign 'Deep Lore,' rather than being processed by centralized corporate trackers. The forom stack, utilizing React 19 and Vite 7, renders these exchanges through a spatial 5x5 viewport, providing a clear visual context for how the software's identity is being refined. This local hosting on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson is a core pillar of the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy, where the community owns the physical keys to its own digital environment. Economically, completing the F1 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP. This incentive structure is vital for kickstarting the internal economy and redistributing purchasing power, especially for the 'Social' role (Blue alignment) whose primary function is to facilitate these very networking and feedback activities. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, F1 is the mechanism that ensures the software remains tailored to the practical needs of student organizations. Every entry at coordinate F1 becomes a permanent part of the V1 Archive, providing the context that ROM, the local RAG engine, will use in Phase 3 to understand the rationale behind software changes. Ultimately, F1 transforms individual suggestions into a collective mandate for technical excellence, securing the node’s functional integrity for the decade ahead. This is the path to digital autonomy. Our software is our legacy. Yes. Finalized. Participating in this exchange, members ensure user interface is intuitive and that the underlying logic remains transparent and robust. This collaborative process is the final safeguard against technical obsolescence in our sovereign node. Yes.
summary: This quest facilitates the collective exchange and refinement of the forom's software design at coordinate F1, ensuring mission-critical usability and sovereign functional integrity.
